title: Des idées pour taxer l'intelligence artificielle
gist: AI is making some people very rich while threatening others' jobs — and if AI replaces workers, the government loses the taxes those wages paid. So proposals to tax AI are multiplying: the state taking a share of AI companies (Bernie Sanders and, vaguely, Trump), taxing the use of AI, or taxing what people consume. Beneath them all sits an unresolved question — what is the actual goal? — and a sci-fi worry about AI no one can control.

body.fr: L'{{intelligence artificielle (l'IA)|artificial intelligence (AI)}} rend certaines personnes très {{riches|rich}}.

bd.meaning: Artificial intelligence makes some people very rich.
chunks: artificial intelligence — the adjective comes AFTER the noun
makes … very rich (rendre + adjective = to make someone ___)
bd.key: rendre + adjective = 'to make ___'. rend riches = 'makes rich'. Note riches adds -s to match personnes.

body.fr: Les grandes {{entreprises|companies}} d'IA {{valent|are worth}} beaucoup d'argent.

bd.meaning: The big AI companies are worth a lot of money.
chunks: the big AI companies — grandes comes BEFORE the noun
are worth a lot of money (valoir = to be worth)
bd.key: grand goes BEFORE the noun. les grandes entreprises = 'the big companies' — one of the small before-the-noun club.

body.fr: Mais l'IA peut aussi prendre le travail des gens.

bd.meaning: But AI can also take people's jobs.
bd.key: Helper + plain verb. peut (can) carries the subject; prendre stays plain.

body.fr: Sans travail, les gens ne {{paient|pay}} pas d'{{impôts|taxes}}.

bd.meaning: Without work, people don't pay taxes.
chunks: people don't pay — the ne…pas sandwich around paient
bd.key: A negative turns 'des' into 'de'. After ne…pas, des impôts becomes d'impôts — the same shift as un → de.
de l' = 'some'. Before argent (money) French adds de l' where English says nothing.

bd.meaning: Many people want to tax AI.
chunks: to tax AI — taxer stays plain after veulent
bd.key: Helper + plain verb. veulent (want) carries the plural subject; taxer stays plain.

body.fr: Mais comment ? Il y a beaucoup d'{{idées|ideas}}.

bd.meaning: But how? There are many ideas.
chunks: there are many ideas (beaucoup de → beaucoup d')
bd.key: il y a = 'there is / are'. il y a beaucoup d'idées = 'there are many ideas'.

body.fr: Première idée : le gouvernement prend une {{partie|part}} des entreprises.

bd.meaning: First idea: the government takes a part of the companies.
chunks: first idea (première comes before the noun and agrees, feminine)
a part of the companies (des = de + les)
bd.key: des = de + les. une partie des entreprises = 'a part of the companies' — the two little words merge.

body.fr: Le {{sénateur|senator}} Bernie Sanders veut une {{taxe|tax}} pour ça.

bd.meaning: Senator Bernie Sanders wants a tax for that.
chunks: a tax for that (ça = that / this)
bd.key: ça = 'that / this'. A short everyday word: pour ça = 'for that'.

body.fr: Le président Trump veut aussi une {{part|share}} pour l'{{État|state}}.

bd.meaning: President Trump also wants a share for the state.
chunks: a share for the state (l'État = the government / state)
bd.key: Reusing the same verb. veut ('wants') serves both Sanders and Trump — French reuses the high-frequency verb rather than reaching for a synonym.

body.fr: Deuxième idée : {{taxer l'usage|tax the use}} de l'IA.

bd.meaning: Second idea: tax the use of AI.
chunks: to tax the use of AI (l'usage = the use)
bd.key: The plain verb as a heading. taxer ('to tax') stands alone here — the bare, unconjugated form, like English 'to tax'.

body.fr: Comme ça, l'IA {{coûte plus cher|costs more}}.

chunks: AI costs more (coûter cher = to be expensive)
bd.key: plus cher = 'more expensive'. plus + adjective builds the comparison — the mirror of moins cher ('cheaper').

body.fr: Et les gens l'{{utilisent moins|use it less}}.

chunks: use it less (l' = 'it', sits before the verb; moins = less)
bd.key: The object goes BEFORE the verb. l'utilisent = 'use it' — French puts the little 'it' (l') in front of the verb, not after.

body.fr: Troisième idée : une taxe sur les {{achats|purchases}}.

bd.meaning: Third idea: a tax on purchases.
chunks: a tax on purchases (les achats = shopping / purchases)
bd.key: sur = 'on'. une taxe sur les achats = 'a tax on purchases'.

body.fr: Quand on {{achète|buys}} une chose, on paie un peu plus.

bd.meaning: When you buy something, you pay a little more.
chunks: when you buy a thing (on = the general 'you / people')
bd.key: on = 'you / people / we'. The workhorse for people in general: on achète, on paie = 'you buy, you pay'.

body.fr: Mais quel est le {{but|goal}} de ces taxes ?

bd.meaning: But what is the goal of these taxes?
chunks: but what is the goal (quel est…? = what is…?)
bd.key: Quel est… ? = 'What is… ?' for asking about a thing: quel est le but ? = 'what is the goal?'

body.fr: Mais les gens ne sont pas toujours {{d'accord|in agreement}}.

chunks: don't always agree — ne…pas wraps sont; être d'accord = to agree
bd.key: être d'accord = 'to agree'. French says 'to be in agreement': sont d'accord. Here ne…pas makes it 'do not agree'.

body.fr: Et il y a une grande {{peur|fear}}.

bd.meaning: And there is a big fear.
chunks: a big fear — grande comes before the noun and agrees (feminine)
bd.key: grand agrees too. Before a feminine noun it becomes grande: une grande peur = 'a big fear'.

body.fr: Si l'IA {{devient|becomes}} trop {{puissante|powerful}}, c'est un très gros problème.

bd.meaning: If AI becomes too powerful, it's a very big problem.
chunks: if AI becomes too powerful (devenir = to become; puissante is feminine to match l'IA)
bd.key: si = 'if'. si l'IA devient… = 'if AI becomes…' — it sets up a condition, just like English 'if'.

grammar.fr: l'IA peut prendre / les gens veulent taxer
grammar.en: helper + plain verb. peut, veulent carry the subject; the second verb (prendre, taxer) stays plain. Your pouvoir / vouloir / devoir family.

grammar.fr: ne paient pas d'impôts / ne sont pas d'accord
grammar.en: the ne … pas sandwich — and des → de. It wraps the verb, and after it des impôts becomes d'impôts.

grammar.fr: les grandes entreprises / une grande peur / un gros problème
grammar.en: a few adjectives come BEFORE the noun. grand, gros, premier sit in front — and still agree: grande, grandes.

quiz.q: Pourquoi le gouvernement peut perdre de l'argent avec l'IA ?
quiz.a: Parce que l'IA peut prendre le travail des gens ; et sans travail, les gens ne paient pas d'impôts. (Because AI can take people's jobs; and without work, people don't pay taxes.)

flag: A long, detailed piece compressed hard to A1 — a neutral overview, not my own view on taxing AI. I kept three of the proposals (the state taking a share, taxing AI's use, and taxing what people buy) and dropped the rest: the specific company valuations, the token / data-centre / capital-tax distinctions, the named economists, and the sovereign-wealth-fund detail. Two word choices to flag: I used taxer / une taxe throughout because it's transparent for you, though French often uses imposer / un impôt for government taxation (I did use impôts for what people pay); and l'usage de l'IA is clear, though l'utilisation de l'IA is just as common.
